name: Decagon
description: Decagon is an approach developed for modeling polypharmacy side effects. It constructs a multimodal graph encompassing protein-protein interactions, drug-protein target interactions, and polypharmacy side effects (represented as distinct edge types of drug-drug interactions). The model uses a new graph convolutional neural network designed for multirelational link prediction within these multimodal networks. Unlike existing approaches that predict simple drug-drug interaction values, Decagon can predict the precise side effect by which a given drug combination clinically manifests. The model demonstrated accurate prediction of polypharmacy side effects, outperforming baselines by up to 69%. Furthermore, it automatically learns representations of side effects indicating co-occurrence and models molecularly based side effects particularly well while maintaining good performance on non-molecular side effects through effective sharing of model parameters across edge types. Decagon provides opportunities to utilize large pharmacogenomic and patient population data to flag and prioritize polypharmacy side effects for further formal pharmacological studies.
